gaps.snippet: nodule cli.bin.myc;
gaps.reason: derived nodule path `cli.bin.myc` has segment(s) [bin] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule cli.bin.myc;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `cli.bin_kw.myc`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet:
fn usage () -> ExitCode { eprintln ! ("usage:\n  \
         myc init  <name>\n  \
         myc build [--config <manifest>] [--unbounded]\n  \
         myc check [--config <manifest>]\n  \
         myc test  [--config <manifest>]\n  \
         myc run   [--config <manifest>] [--unbounded]  # single- or multi-nodule (M-908/M-909)\n  \
         myc --stream [<file>]\n\
         \n  \
         --unbounded  opt-in, NON-DETERMINISTIC: lift the recursion-depth ceiling (RFC-0041 §5).\n               \
         Machine-dependent; excluded from the conformance corpus; never for CI/reproducible builds.") ; ExitCode :: from (64) }
gaps.reason: function body contains a macro-invocation statement (e.g. `debug_assert!`/`println!`) — no macro system in this grammar fragment

("next: cd {name} && myc check") ; ExitCode :: SUCCESS } Err (r) => fail (& r) , } , _ => usage () , } , "build" => with_run_options (& rest , "build" , | m , _opts | cmd_build (m)) , "check" => with_manifest (& rest , cmd_check) , "test" => with_manifest (& rest , cmd_test) , "run" => with_run_options (& rest , "run" , | m , opts | match run_with_options (m , opts) { Ok (report) => { println ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Resolve the `--config <manifest>` flag (default `mycelium-proj.toml`) and dispatch. Used by"] # [doc = " `check`/`test`, which do **not** accept `--unbounded` (RFC-0041 §5: CLI-flag-only, and only on the"] # [doc = " execution/build drivers) — so an `--unbounded` here is an unknown flag (usage error)."] fn with_manifest (rest : & [String] , f : impl FnOnce (& Path) -> ExitCode) -> ExitCode { let mut manifest = PathBuf :: from ("mycelium-proj.toml") ; let mut it = rest . iter () ; while let Some (a) = it . next () { match a . as_str () { "--config" => match it . next () { Some (p) => manifest = PathBuf :: from (p) , None => return usage () , } , _ => return usage () , } } f (& manifest) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Resolve `--config <manifest>` **and** the RFC-0041 §5 `--unbounded` flag for `run`/`build`, then"] # [doc = " dispatch. When `--unbounded` is engaged: (1) if a conformance-corpus / CI context is signalled"] # [doc = " ([`corpus_context`]), it is **refused** never-silently ([`reject_unbounded_in_corpus`]) — the"] # [doc = " deterministic corpus path must not run the machine-dependent mode; (2) otherwise a never-silent"] # [doc = " banner ([`unbounded_banner`]) is printed to stderr before dispatch (G2). `cmd` (`\"run\"`/`\"build\"`)"] # [doc = " tailors the banner's per-command effect line."] fn with_run_options (rest : & [String] , cmd : & str , f : impl FnOnce (& Path , & RunOptions) -> ExitCode ,) -> ExitCode { let mut manifest = PathBuf :: from ("mycelium-proj.toml") ; let mut opts = RunOptions :: default () ; let mut it = rest . iter () ; while let Some (a) = it . next () { match a . as_str () { "--config" => match it . next () { Some (p) => manifest = PathBuf :: from (p) , None => return usage () , } , "--unbounded" => opts . unbounded = true , _ => return usage () , } } if opts . unbounded { if corpus_context () { if let Err (r) = reject_unbounded_in_corpus (& opts) { return fail (& r) ; } } eprintln ! ("{}" , unbounded_banner (cmd)) ; } f (& manifest , & opts) }
gaps.reason: unsupported Rust type form `impl FnOnce (& Path , & RunOptions) -> ExitCode`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " `myc --stream [<file>]` — parse a `;`-terminated Mycelium component stream (M-820 / DN-57)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Without a file argument, reads from stdin (`<stdin>`). With a file argument, opens and reads"] # [doc = " that file. The source is lexed once and the token stream is segmented at `nodule` header tokens"] # [doc = " into per-nodule components, each parsed with `mycelium_l1::parse`. The split is token-driven,"] # [doc = " so it is comment-/string-safe by construction (a `nodule`/`;` inside a comment is never a token;"] # [doc = " DN-57 §2). v0 I/O is whole-input-buffered (`Declared` — see [`mycelium_cli::stream_parse`])."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every malformed component surfaces an explicit error with a component:line:col location (G2)."] # [doc = " An unterminated component (its last item has no `;` before the next `nodule`/EOF) is likewise an"] # [doc = " explicit error, never a silent partial accept (G2 / DN-57 §3.1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Exit 0 on all-green; exit 65 if any component failed (or on lex error); exit 66 on I/O error."] fn cmd_stream (rest : & [String]) -> ExitCode { let (reader , source_name) : (Box < dyn std :: io :: Read > , String) = match rest { [] => (Box :: new (std :: io :: stdin ()) , "<stdin>" . to_owned ()) , [path] if ! path . starts_with ('-') => match std :: fs :: File :: open (path) { Ok (f) => (Box :: new (f) , path . clone ()) , Err (e) => { let r = Report :: new ("myc-stream-io" , format ! ("{path}: {e}") , 66) . help ("check that the file path is correct and the file is readable") ; return fail (& r) ; } } , _ => return usage () , } ; match run_stream_parse (reader , & source_name) { Err (r) => fail (& r) , Ok (report) => { for f in & report . failures { eprintln ! ("{}\n" , f . render ()) ; } if report . ok () { eprintln ! ("myc: stream `{}` — {} component(s) parsed clean" , report . source_name , report . parsed_ok ,) ; ExitCode :: SUCCESS } else { eprintln ! ("myc: stream `{}` — {} ok, {} failed" , report . source_name , report . parsed_ok , report . parsed_err ,) ; ExitCode :: from (65) } } } }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)
